title: Artifact Lookup (artifact-lookup v1.0)
description: Request/response contract for the artifact_lookup endpoint. Enables first-class addressability of query runtime artifacts (query_trace, context_bundle, agent_query_session) by stable ID.

artifact_type.description: The type of runtime artifact to retrieve.

id.description: The artifact ID returned at query time. Stable within the local QueryArtifactStore for the lifetime of the store; not guaranteed across service restarts that change the store location.

source_query.description: The query text that produced this artifact.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of artifact creation.

index_id.description: The build artifact ID (index) used to produce this artifact.

run_id.description: Optional correlation ID linking related artifacts from the same query execution.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this artifact was stored.

data.description: The artifact payload. Structure depends on artifact_type.

authority.description: Authority class of this runtime artifact. Always 'runtime_observation' for query runtime artifacts.

canonicality.description: Canonicality class. Always 'observation' for query runtime artifacts.

artifact_shape.description: Shape of the stored artifact data: 'raw' = internal execute_query form (query_trace), 'projected' = API-projected form after output-profile filtering (context_bundle), 'wrapper' = session wrapper built from the projected form (agent_query_session).

retention_policy.description: Retention policy for this artifact in the store. 'unbounded_currently' means no GC is applied.

lifecycle_status.description: Lifecycle status of this runtime artifact. 'active' means the artifact is present and not expired. No GC or TTL is applied currently.

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp (ISO 8601) if a TTL is set; null while retention_policy is 'unbounded_currently'.

does_not_prove.description: Statements this artifact does not prove.

claim_boundaries.description: Epistemic boundaries of this runtime artifact.

artifact_type.description: The artifact type that was requested.

id.description: The artifact ID that was requested.

status.description: Lookup outcome. 'ok' means artifact was found and returned. 'not_found' means no artifact exists with this id/type. 'error' means an internal failure occurred.

artifact.description: The artifact payload including provenance. Null when status is not 'ok'.

warnings.description: Non-fatal diagnostic messages (e.g. type mismatch, store not initialized).
